Title: Verify that opening a deep link to the map highlights the relevant vehicle entity correctly (AC-MAP-13.1)
Preconditions: User has access to the dispatcher portal - A valid deep link URL for a specific vehicle entity exists - The vehicle is currently active on the map - User is logged in with appropriate RBAC role
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the map via the deep link URL containing the vehicle ID - 3- Observe the map view upon load - 4- Verify the targeted vehicle is highlighted or selected on the map
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map opens via deep link - 3- Map displays a filtered or focused view - 4- The specific vehicle referenced in the deep link is highlighted or visually distinguished from other markers on the map

Title: Verify that opening a deep link to the map highlights the relevant No-Run passenger entity correctly (AC-MAP-13.1)
Preconditions: User has access to the dispatcher portal - A valid deep link URL for a specific No-Run passenger exists - The No-Run passenger marker is present on the map - User is logged in with appropriate RBAC role
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the map via the deep link URL referencing a No-Run passenger - 3- Observe the map view upon load - 4- Verify the No-Run passenger marker is highlighted or selected
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map opens via deep link - 3- Map displays a filtered view with the No-Run passenger in focus - 4- The specific No-Run passenger referenced in the deep link is highlighted - other entities are visible but not in focus

Title: Verify that an alert surface supports deep linking to the map and redirects to the relevant vehicle filtered view
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active alert exists referencing a specific vehicle - Alert panel is visible - User has map access
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the alerts panel - 3- Click the deep link within an alert that references a specific vehicle - 4- Observe the resulting map view
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Alerts panel is displayed with active alerts - 3- Deep link within alert is clickable - 4- Map opens in a filtered view with the referenced vehicle highlighted - no unrelated vehicles are pre-selected

Title: Verify that other UI surfaces (non-alert) support deep linking to the map with the relevant vehicle highlighted
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - A UI surface other than alerts (e.g. trip list panel or run detail) contains a deep link to the map for a specific vehicle - User has map access
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to a non-alert UI surface (e.g. trip list or run detail panel) - 3- Click the deep link that references a specific vehicle on the map - 4- Observe the resulting map view
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Non-alert UI surface is displayed - 3- Deep link to map is clickable - 4- Map opens with the referenced vehicle highlighted in a filtered view

Title: Verify that the map provides a functional deep link to the Run detail page when RBAC permits (AC-MAP-13.2)
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has RBAC permission to view Run details - A vehicle or run marker is visible on the map - Hover/click popup is accessible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a role that has Run view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a vehicle or run marker to open the hover/click popup - 4- Click the Run deep link in the popup - 5- Observe the navigation target
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map is displayed with vehicle/run markers - 3- Popup opens showing Run ID as a clickable link - 4- Clicking the Run deep link navigates to the Run detail page - 5- Run detail page loads correctly with relevant data

Title: Verify that the map provides a functional deep link to the Trip detail page when RBAC permits (AC-MAP-13.2)
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has RBAC permission to view Trip details - A trip marker is visible on the map - Hover/click popup is accessible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a role that has Trip view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a trip marker to open the hover/click popup - 4- Click the Trip deep link in the popup - 5- Observe the navigation target
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map is displayed with trip markers - 3- Popup opens showing Trip ID as a clickable link - 4- Clicking the Trip deep link navigates to the Trip detail page - 5- Trip detail page loads correctly

Title: Verify that the map provides a functional deep link to the Vehicle detail page when RBAC permits (AC-MAP-13.2)
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has RBAC permission to view Vehicle details - A vehicle marker is visible on the map - Hover/click popup is accessible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a role that has Vehicle view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a vehicle marker to open the hover/click popup - 4- Click the Vehicle deep link in the popup - 5- Observe the navigation target
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map is displayed with vehicle markers - 3- Popup opens showing Vehicle ID as a clickable link - 4- Clicking the Vehicle deep link navigates to the Vehicle detail page - 5- Vehicle detail page loads correctly

Title: Verify that the map provides a functional deep link to the Client detail page when RBAC permits (AC-MAP-13.2)
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has RBAC permission to view Client details - A passenger/client marker is visible on the map - Hover/click popup is accessible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a role that has Client view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a passenger or client marker to open the hover/click popup - 4- Click the Client deep link in the popup - 5- Observe the navigation target
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map is displayed with client markers - 3- Popup opens showing Client ID as a clickable link - 4- Clicking the Client deep link navigates to the Client detail page - 5- Client detail page loads correctly

Title: Verify that deep link to map with a vehicle ID fails gracefully when the vehicle is no longer active or does not exist
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - A deep link URL is constructed with a vehicle ID that has been deactivated or removed from the system
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the map using a deep link URL containing a non-existent or deactivated vehicle ID - 3- Observe the map behavior and any error messaging
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map opens via deep link - 3- System displays a graceful error message or empty filtered state (e.g. "Vehicle not found" or "Entity unavailable") - no crash or unhandled exception occurs

Title: Verify that navigating to the map via a deep link with a malformed or invalid URL does not crash the application
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An intentionally malformed deep link URL (e.g. missing entity ID - invalid parameters - garbled query string) is used to access the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the map using a malformed deep link URL - 3- Observe the application behavior
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map deep link is attempted with invalid URL - 3- Application displays a graceful error or redirects to the default map view - no crash - no unhandled exception - a user-friendly message is shown if applicable

Title: Verify that the hover/click popup on the map includes the required entity IDs as part of the deep link entry points (AC-MAP-13.2)
Preconditions: User is logged in with full RBAC permissions - Multiple entity types (Run - Trip - Vehicle - Client) are visible on the live map - Hover/click functionality is enabled
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with full permissions - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Hover over and then click on a vehicle marker - 4- Inspect the popup content for the presence of Run - Trip - Vehicle and Client IDs as deep-link entry points
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map is displayed - 3- Popup opens on hover/click - 4- Popup contains the Run ID - Trip ID - Vehicle ID and Client ID (where applicable) as clickable deep links or visible identifiers consistent with RBAC

Title: Verify that the deep link opens the map in a filtered view showing only the referenced entity and does not show all entities unfiltered
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - A valid deep link URL for a specific vehicle is used - Multiple vehicles and runs are active on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the map via the deep link referencing a specific vehicle ID - 3- Observe whether the map opens in an unfiltered view or a focused/filtered view
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Map opens via deep link - 3- Map displays in a focused or filtered state with the referenced vehicle highlighted - the view is not simply the default full-map view with all entities unfiltered

Title: Verify that the back-navigation from a deep-linked map view returns the user to the originating surface (alert or UI panel)
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - User navigated to the map via a deep link from the alerts panel - User is now on the deep-linked map view
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Open the alerts panel and click a deep link to navigate to the map - 3- On the map view - use the browser back button or in-app back navigation - 4- Observe where the user is returned to
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Alerts panel deep link navigates to map - 3- Back navigation is triggered - 4- User is returned to the alerts panel or the originating UI surface - no broken navigation or blank page

Title: Verify deep link behavior is consistent when user is not logged in and the deep link is accessed directly via URL
Preconditions: User is NOT logged in - A valid deep link URL to the map with a vehicle entity ID is accessed directly via the browser address bar
Steps(Step): 1- Open the browser without logging in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Paste a valid map deep link URL directly into the browser address bar and navigate - 3- Observe the application behavior
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Browser navigates to the deep link URL - 2- Application detects unauthenticated access - 3- User is redirected to the login page - 4- After successful login the deep link destination is loaded with the referenced entity highlighted

Title: Verify that the map deep link URL is shareable and produces the same filtered view when opened by another authorized user
Preconditions: User A is logged in as Dispatcher and has navigated to a deep-linked map view - User B has a valid account with appropriate RBAC permissions - Both users have the same role
Steps(Step): 1- User A navigates to the map via a deep link showing a specific vehicle - 2- User A copies the current browser URL - 3- User B opens the copied URL in their browser and logs in - 4- User B observes the map view
Steps(Expected Result): 1- User A lands on map deep link view - 2- URL is copied - 3- User B logs in and navigates to the URL - 4- User B sees the same filtered map view with the same vehicle highlighted - deep link is shareable and role-consistent

Title: Verify that the mini-map feasibility concept renders a map widget inline within a non-map page without breaking the page layout
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - A UI page (e.g. trip detail or run detail) includes a mini-map widget inline - The mini-map is configured to display the relevant vehicle or entity
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to a detail page that includes an inline mini-map widget - 3- Observe whether the mini-map renders correctly within the page - 4- Verify the mini-map shows the relevant entity and does not break the surrounding page layout
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Detail page with mini-map is displayed - 3- Mini-map widget renders inline within the page without overflowing or disrupting the surrounding layout - 4- Relevant vehicle or entity is visible on the mini-map

Title: Verify deep link to map functions correctly across desktop and tablet screen sizes
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - A valid deep link URL for a vehicle entity exists - Application is accessed on desktop and tablet screen sizes
Steps(Step): 1- Log in on a desktop browser and open the map via deep link - verify the entity is highlighted and layout is correct - 2- Repeat on a tablet-sized browser viewport - 3- Compare the deep-linked map view across both sizes
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Desktop view: deep link opens map with entity highlighted - layout is correct and no overflow - 2- Tablet view: deep link opens map with entity highlighted - map and popup layout adapts responsively - 3- Core deep link functionality (entity highlight and popup) works on both screen sizes

Title: Verify that the deep link entry points in the popup are rendered only for entity types the user has RBAC permission to view (AC-MAP-13.2)
Preconditions: User is logged in with a restricted RBAC role that permits viewing Vehicle and Trip but not Client or Run - A vehicle marker is visible on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a restricted RBAC role (Vehicle and Trip view only) - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a vehicle marker to open the hover/click popup - 4- Inspect the popup for the presence or absence of Run - Trip - Vehicle and Client deep-link entry points
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with restricted role - 2- Map is displayed - 3- Popup opens on click - 4- Popup shows Trip ID and Vehicle ID as clickable deep links - Run ID and Client ID are either not shown or shown as plain non-clickable text consistent with RBAC restrictions
